In 2016, the Pocoo team was disbanded and Flask development moved to The Pallets Projects, a community-driven open-source organization. Flask is now one of the most widely used Python web frameworks globally.
Flask.summary_abuse: Flask is a foundational Python web framework with no abuse surface of its own. As a server-side library, it has no consumer-facing exposure. Like any web framework, applications built with it can have vulnerabilities if developers do not follow security best practices, but Flask itself is not a vector for platform-level abuse.

Flat UI.history: Flat UI is a Bootstrap-based UI theme and design framework created by Designmodo in 2013. It applies flat design aesthetics to Bootstrap components. Released as open source, it gained widespread adoption during the flat design trend peak around 2013-2015. Development has since become dormant as Bootstrap itself evolved.
Flat UI.summary_abuse: Flat UI is a dormant CSS/JS design library with no abuse surface. It is a frontend styling framework with no server-side components or data collection.

Flazio.history: Flazio was launched in 2012 in Catania, Sicily by siblings Elisa and Flavio Fazio (the name is a portmanteau of Flavio and Fazio). It is described as Italy's first website builder. The platform raised $400,000 from Zernike Meta Venture and in 2015 partnered with Telecom Italia, Dada Group, Register, and Tiscali for white-label reselling. Elisa Fazio appeared on Forbes list of 100 successful female founders in 2016. The platform has hosted 500,000 websites.
Flazio.summary_abuse: Flazio is a free website builder that allows users to create sites under its domain or custom domains. As a hosting and website creation platform with free tier access, it is occasionally used to create spam or low-quality sites, which is common for all free website builders. The platform is primarily a legitimate Italian web builder with no specific documented large-scale abuse incidents.

Fleeq.history: Fleeq officially launched in January 2018 as a SaaS video and explainer content creation platform. The product evolved to include course building and knowledge base capabilities. The company has used strategic pivots to grow revenue and primarily serves SMB customers for employee and customer onboarding.
Fleeq.summary_abuse: Fleeq is a B2B video onboarding platform with no documented abuse history. As a tool for creating training videos for internal use, it has no notable consumer-facing attack surface.

Fleetee.history: Fleetee is a cloud-based multi-tenant fleet management SaaS platform providing vehicle tracking, maintenance scheduling, fuel management, and driver management for businesses operating vehicle fleets. The platform serves small to medium-sized businesses needing centralized fleet operations. Limited public information is available about the company's founding year and ownership.
Fleetee.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Fleetee is a small fleet management SaaS provider with limited public profile. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported. GDPR compliance is expected for a European-based company.

Fleksa.history: Fleksa is a German restaurant management SaaS platform offering an all-in-one AI-powered operating system for restaurants. The platform provides website, online ordering (zero commission), cloud POS, reservations, kitchen display, and marketing tools. It integrates with major food delivery platforms like Lieferando, Uber Eats, and Wolt. Positioned as an alternative to commission-heavy aggregators, allowing restaurants to own their customer relationships.
Fleksa.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Fleksa is a legitimate restaurant management SaaS. As a German company it operates under GDPR. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Flexbe.history: Flexbe is a no-code website and landing page builder platform that has grown to serve 35,000+ active sites. The platform provides drag-and-drop editing, ecommerce capabilities, 30+ page elements, SEO tools, and integrations with marketing platforms. It was rebuilt with AI-driven features for smarter content creation and optimization. The platform competes with tools like Wix, Squarespace, and Landingi.
Flexbe.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Flexbe is a straightforward website builder with a freemium model. No dark patterns or data misuse reported. Operates under GDPR as a European company.

FlexCMP.history: FlexCMP is an Italian PHP/MySQL content management system founded in 2002 in Castenaso, Italy. It is an unfunded, independent CMS with fewer than 100 live websites. The product is niche and primarily used by Italian businesses.
FlexCMP.summary_abuse: FlexCMP is a niche Italian CMS with a negligible install base and no documented abuse incidents. As a self-hosted CMS, abuse risk lies with how individual sites are configured rather than with the platform itself.

FlexiFunnels.history: FlexiFunnels is India's first sales funnel builder, launched on August 15, 2021. Founded to serve Indian digital entrepreneurs, agencies, and course creators, it integrates with Indian payment gateways including Instamojo, Razorpay, and PayU alongside global ones like Stripe and PayPal. Features include drag-and-drop page building, LMS/course hosting, upsells, downsells, cart abandonment recovery, and 300+ templates.
FlexiFunnels.summary_abuse: No major abuse or security incidents identified. FlexiFunnels is primarily an India-focused funnel builder with aggressive marketing typical of the segment. The platform collects payment and customer data on behalf of merchants. No formal security certifications or GDPR compliance are advertised.

Flexmls.history: Flexmls is a leading MLS (Multiple Listing Service) software platform developed by FBS, based in Fargo, North Dakota. FBS is 100% employee-owned and one of the three largest MLS technology providers in the US with over 45 years of operation. Flexmls serves 300,000+ real estate agents and integrates with 70+ third-party tools. It is deployed by major MLS organizations including CRMLS and provides mobile-first MLS search, listing management, and client communication tools.
Flexmls.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Flexmls/FBS is a well-established, employee-owned company with 45+ years of history in real estate technology. It operates as regulated MLS infrastructure. No data misuse or compliance violations reported.

FlexSlider.history: FlexSlider is a responsive jQuery slider plugin originally created by WooThemes and published in 2011. It was widely adopted as a standard image/content carousel for WordPress sites. After WooThemes was acquired by Automattic (parent of WooCommerce), the repository moved to the WooCommerce GitHub organization. It remains in use on many existing sites despite newer alternatives.
FlexSlider.summary_abuse: FlexSlider is a client-side jQuery carousel library with no abuse surface. It is static JavaScript code included in web pages with no data collection or server-side exposure.

FlexyPe.history: FlexyPe is an Indian checkout infrastructure startup providing one-click checkout for D2C e-commerce brands. The platform powers over INR 200 Cr in annualized GMV across 300+ merchants and 20M+ shoppers. Key features include local payment methods (UPI, wallets, BNPL), COD risk verification, cart abandonment reduction, and cross-border checkout. It aims to reduce RTO by 70% and boost conversions by 60% through optimized checkout flows.
FlexyPe.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. FlexyPe handles e-commerce checkout data for Indian merchants. As a payments infrastructure company it deals with sensitive transaction data. No data breaches or regulatory violations reported. Early-stage company with limited compliance certifications.

Flickity.history: Flickity is a touch-responsive, flickable carousel JavaScript library created by David DeSandro of Metafizzy, released around 2015. It is widely used for image carousels and galleries on commercial websites. Metafizzy also created Isotope and Masonry. Commercial licenses are available alongside the GPL v3 open source license.
Flickity.summary_abuse: Flickity is a client-side JavaScript carousel library with no abuse surface. It has no data collection or server-side components.

Flip.history: Flip was a social commerce app for beauty and wellness products founded in 2019 by Nooruldeen Agha in Los Angeles. The app allowed users to buy products and post video reviews or live shopping shows, earning commissions on sales. It raised $28M Series A led by Streamlined Ventures in August 2021 and was once valued at over $1 billion. However the company shut down abruptly without notifying creators and brands, unable to compete with TikTok Shop.
Flip.summary_abuse: Flip shut down abruptly without warning to its creator partners and brand merchants, causing financial harm to those who relied on the platform. The company raised significant VC funding but failed to compete with TikTok Shop. The sudden shutdown without notice to affected parties is considered a significant trust violation.

Flip-Pay.history: Flip-Pay is an Irish digital publishing monetization platform founded in 2015, based in Dublin. The company specializes in dynamic paywalls, subscription management, and content monetization for news publishers, video, podcasts, and rich media streaming. Products include INTELLIGATE (intelligent paywall with A/B testing) and RENEWA (subscription management with anti-churn features). It serves publishers needing end-to-end revenue management for digital and print products.
Flip-Pay.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Flip-Pay is a legitimate B2B SaaS for publisher monetization and paywalls. As an Irish company it is subject to GDPR. Handles payment data so PCI compliance is expected. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

FlipBuilder.history: FlipBuilder is a Chinese software company offering HTML5 flipbook maker software that converts PDFs and images into interactive page-flip publications. The main product Flip PDF Plus Pro allows embedding multimedia, YouTube, and interactive elements. Published content can be output as HTML, EXE, APP, or APK. A free tier is available and paid licenses offer lifetime access. The company serves marketers, educators, publishers, and businesses worldwide.
FlipBuilder.summary_abuse: No major abuse incidents identified. FlipBuilder is a Chinese-developed desktop software. GDPR compliance and formal security certifications are not prominently advertised. The company has been active for many years with no notable data breach or scam history, though the Chinese origin means some data governance uncertainty.

FlipClock.js.history: FlipClock.js is an open source JavaScript library for building flip-clock style visual elements including clocks, countdown timers, counters, and scoreboards. Written in TypeScript with full unit test coverage, it is framework-agnostic and works with React, Vue, Angular, Svelte, and Solid. Originally created around 2012 and maintained by objectivehtml on GitHub, it is available via npm and cdnjs. Licensed under MIT.
FlipClock.js.summary_abuse: No abuse or security concerns identified. FlipClock.js is a simple open source UI library with MIT license. It has no tracking, telemetry, or data collection. No security incidents reported.

Flipdish.history: Flipdish was founded in 2015 in Dublin, Ireland by brothers Conor and James McCarthy. The company provides an all-in-one online ordering and POS platform for restaurants and takeaways. It offers branded websites, mobile apps, self-service kiosks, and delivery integrations. The platform is primarily focused on the UK and Irish restaurant markets.
Flipdish.summary_abuse: Flipdish is a restaurant ordering platform that processes customer payment and personal data. As a food ordering service for legitimate restaurants, it has no notable abuse history. Standard payment security practices apply.

Flipping Pro CRM.history: Flipping Pro CRM is a US-based real estate marketing CRM built specifically for house flippers and real estate investors. The platform offers integrated communications including calls, SMS, and email at usage-based rates, along with lead management, team permissions, and Zapier integration via LeadConnector. It replaces multiple separate tools for investor communications and deal tracking. No long-term contracts are required.
Flipping Pro CRM.summary_abuse: No specific abuse or security incidents identified. Flipping Pro is a niche CRM for real estate investors with limited public profile. The platform handles lead and contact data. No formal compliance certifications advertised. Limited user reviews available to assess quality.

FlippingBook.history: FlippingBook is a digital publishing platform founded around 2009, headquartered in Estonia. The company offers cloud SaaS (FlippingBook Online) and desktop software (FlippingBook Publisher) for converting PDFs into interactive HTML5 flipbooks with page-flip effects. Used by manufacturers, marketers, real estate agents, educators, and insurance brokers. The platform supports analytics tracking for documents and includes features like video embedding, lead capture forms, and brand customization.
FlippingBook.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. FlippingBook is a long-established digital publishing SaaS with transparent pricing and good reputation. As an Estonian company it operates under GDPR. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Flipshop.history: Flipshop was an Indian e-commerce website builder allowing businesses to create online stores without technical skills, targeting Indian sellers with localized payment options and regional language support. The platform offered drag-and-drop design, AI-generated product descriptions, and integrations with Google Ads and Facebook Pixel. The service was officially discontinued on March 31, 2025, shutting down all operations.
Flipshop.summary_abuse: Flipshop has been officially shut down as of March 31, 2025. Prior to shutdown, the platform served Indian e-commerce merchants. No major abuse incidents documented. The abrupt shutdown may have impacted merchants who relied on the platform for their online stores.

Flipsite.history: Flipsite is a performance-focused website builder that achieves 100% Lighthouse scores for SEO, speed, and accessibility. It offers a free tier with no forced branding, commercial plans from $9/month, and deployment to Cloudflare Pages, GitHub Pages, AWS, Netlify, or FTP. The platform provides drag-and-drop editing, CMS content binding, animations, and multi-language support. Positioned as a developer-friendly but no-code alternative for building fast, accessible websites.
Flipsite.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Flipsite is a small, legitimate website builder focused on performance and privacy. The platform appears to minimize tracker usage. No dark patterns or data misuse reported.

Flipsnack.history: Flipsnack was launched in 2011 as a digital flipbook creator allowing users to convert PDFs into interactive publications. The company is based in Romania and has grown to serve marketing teams and enterprises. It competes with FlippingBook as a digital publishing platform.
Flipsnack.summary_abuse: Flipsnack is a digital flipbook platform used for marketing materials and publications. Like all document hosting platforms, it could theoretically be used to distribute misleading content, but is primarily used for legitimate business documents. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Flits.history: Flits is an Indian Shopify app providing loyalty and rewards programs, customer account pages, wishlists, store credit, referrals, and social login for Shopify merchants. The app has earned 4.8/5 from 650+ reviews on the Shopify App Store. It combines multiple retention-focused features in a single app, allowing merchants to avoid multiple separate plugins. Founded to help Shopify brands increase repeat purchases and lifetime customer value.
Flits.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Flits is a well-reviewed Shopify retention app with a strong track record on the Shopify App Store. It handles customer loyalty data and store credit. As a Shopify-approved app it meets Shopify's marketplace standards. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Floatbot.history: Floatbot was founded in 2017 with a vision to automate customer service through conversational AI. The platform provides no-code AI voice agents, chatbots, email automation, and real-time agent assist for enterprises in insurance, collections, banking, and healthcare. The flagship product Floatbot UNO automates up to 60% of inbound and outbound contact center interactions. The platform expanded into the North American market and integrates with RingCentral and other CCaaS platforms.
Floatbot.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Floatbot is an enterprise conversational AI platform targeting regulated industries including insurance and healthcare. The platform processes customer communications data. No known data breaches or misuse incidents reported.

Floating UI.history: Floating UI is the successor to Popper.js, created by atomiks (Dominic Nguyen) and released around 2021. It provides a low-level JavaScript library for positioning floating elements such as tooltips, popovers, and dropdown menus. Floating UI replaced Popper.js as the de facto standard for UI floating element positioning, with a tiny 600-byte core and platform-agnostic design supporting web and React Native.
Floating UI.summary_abuse: Floating UI is a pure JavaScript positioning utility library with no data collection or server-side exposure. It has no abuse surface.

Flockler.history: Flockler is a Finnish social media aggregation SaaS that embeds social media feeds and walls from 12+ platforms into websites. Founded around 2014, it serves brands, agencies, and enterprises looking to display social proof and UGC on their websites.
Flockler.summary_abuse: Flockler is a social media aggregation tool that embeds third-party social feeds. Social aggregators can surface inappropriate or copyrighted content if moderation is insufficient. The platform provides content moderation tools but relies on platform APIs which may change. No specific abuse incidents documented.

FlockRocket.history: FlockRocket is a customer review management system designed to help businesses collect, manage, and display verified customer feedback. The platform targets e-commerce and local businesses seeking to build reputation through customer reviews. Limited public information is available about the company history, founding team, or investment status.
FlockRocket.summary_abuse: No specific abuse incidents identified. FlockRocket is a reviews platform which could theoretically be used for fake review generation, though no documented cases are found. The platform is small with limited public presence. No GDPR compliance or security certifications are prominently advertised.

Flocktory.history: Flocktory was founded in 2012 in Moscow, Russia as an omnichannel personalization and marketing automation platform. It analyzed over 38% of Russian online transactions at its peak. Qiwi acquired Flocktory in 2017 for $14 million. Following Qiwi's Russian asset divestment in early 2024, Flocktory was transferred to Fusion Factor Fintech Limited and subsequently acquired by Alfa-Bank in April 2024. The platform offers 20+ MarTech solutions including onsite personalization, email retargeting, loyalty, and a CDP.
Flocktory.summary_abuse: Flocktory is a Russian marketing automation platform now owned by Alfa-Bank following Qiwi's 2024 Russian asset sales. Russian ownership raises data sovereignty concerns for non-Russian users. The platform processes significant customer behavioral and transaction data from Russian e-commerce sites. No specific abuse incidents documented, but Russian jurisdiction creates privacy and data governance considerations for EU/US businesses.

Flodesk.history: Flodesk was founded in 2018 by Martha Bitar and Rebecca Shostak in San Francisco. The platform grew virally using a mandatory email footer and affiliate program, reaching $1M ARR in four months without VC funding. It targets small businesses, creators, and solopreneurs with flat-rate pricing regardless of subscriber count.
Flodesk.summary_abuse: Flodesk is an email marketing platform with a flat-rate unlimited subscriber model. Email marketing platforms can be used to send spam, and Flodesk's unlimited subscriber model at a fixed price could theoretically attract high-volume senders. The company enforces anti-spam policies consistent with CAN-SPAM and GDPR requirements. No documented large-scale abuse incidents.

Floori.history: Floori is an AR and AI-powered flooring visualization platform for flooring manufacturers, distributors, and retailers. The platform converts physical samples into AR-ready digital assets, provides room visualizers where customers can see flooring products in their actual space, and includes sales tools for flooring professionals. It serves the flooring, tiles, and walls industry with features like AI surface detection, floor plan creation, and BIM-compatible digital catalogs.
Floori.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Floori is a legitimate B2B SaaS for the flooring industry with specialized AR/AI technology. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported. As a European company it operates under GDPR.

Floranext.history: Floranext is a US-based cloud florist management platform built specifically for the floral industry by former florists. It provides an integrated POS, florist website builder, online ordering, delivery routing with driver app, and floral accounting. The platform targets independent flower shops as an alternative to wire service networks like FTD and Teleflora, allowing florists to keep 100% of order revenue. No commitment contracts or hidden fees.
Floranext.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Floranext is a legitimate industry-specific POS and website platform for florists. Handles payment card data on behalf of florists. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Florist Touch.history: Florist Touch is a UK-based florist website and management platform based in London (Regent Street). The platform provides florist websites, online shop, till mode/POS, delivery routing with driver app, and photo proof of delivery. It targets small independent UK flower shops with a no-contract, low-cost model accessible on all devices including mobile. All data is kept in sync across devices.
Florist Touch.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Florist Touch is a small UK-specific florist management platform. As a UK company it operates under UK GDPR. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Florist Window.history: Florist Window is a UK-based florist website and e-commerce platform based in Cambridge (CB3). The platform provides websites built specifically for florists who sell direct to customers without relay network fees or intermediaries. Features include mobile-enabled sites, delivery route assignment, PayPal integration, and local order management. The company has been building florist websites for over ten years and operates on a direct florist network model at approximately GBP 1,276 per year.
Florist Window.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Florist Window is a small UK florist website platform with over a decade of operation. Operates under UK GDPR. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Flot.history: Flot is a pure JavaScript charting library for jQuery created by Ole Laursen around 2007 and sponsored by IOLA, a Danish web agency. It was widely adopted for interactive web charts before modern alternatives like Chart.js emerged. The project has been largely dormant as newer libraries replaced it.
Flot.summary_abuse: Flot is a client-side jQuery charting library with no data collection or server components. It has no abuse surface.

Flourish.history: Flourish was co-founded in 2018 by Duncan Clark and Robin Houston in London as a data visualization and storytelling platform. The company grew to 1.5 million users and became widely used by journalists, newsrooms, and businesses for interactive data storytelling. Canva acquired Flourish in 2022 to integrate data visualization capabilities into its design platform.
Flourish.summary_abuse: Flourish is a data visualization platform used by journalists and publishers with no documented abuse history. It is a reputable tool acquired by Canva. No notable misuse patterns or security incidents.

Flow.history: Flow is a project and task management SaaS for teams developed by MetaLab Design, a Canadian design company. Launched around 2010, it provides Kanban boards, task timelines, team collaboration, task delegation, and to-do list prioritization. It offers iOS and Android apps alongside the web app. Priced at $8-18 per user/month across three tiers. Positioned as a simpler alternative to Asana and Monday.com for small teams.
Flow.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Flow is a straightforward project management SaaS by an established Canadian design studio. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Flowbite.history: Flowbite is an open-source Tailwind CSS UI component library created by Themesberg and first released around 2021. It fills the gap of pre-built components for Tailwind CSS, similar to Bootstrap's component ecosystem. It includes 400+ interactive components and supports React, Vue, Svelte, and Angular. The library has rapidly become one of the most popular Tailwind CSS component collections.
Flowbite.summary_abuse: Flowbite is a client-side UI component library with no data collection or server-side exposure. It has no abuse surface.

Flowbox.history: Flowbox is a Swedish user-generated content (UGC) platform founded around 2016, headquartered in Stockholm with regional offices in Spain, Denmark, and the Netherlands. The platform enables brands to collect, moderate, and distribute UGC from social media into their ecommerce experiences. It works with 800+ brands globally.
Flowbox.summary_abuse: Flowbox aggregates user-generated social media content for brand use. Platforms that pull social media content could theoretically surface copyrighted or inappropriate material if moderation is insufficient. The company provides content moderation tools and media rights management. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Flowplayer.history: Flowplayer was founded in Finland around 2005 as one of the first widely adopted HTML5 and Flash video players for the web. It became a popular choice for web publishers. The project was later acquired by Wowza Media Systems, a US streaming infrastructure company. The open-source version is available on GitHub alongside the commercial hosted offering.
Flowplayer.summary_abuse: Flowplayer is a legitimate video player used by many websites and publishers. It has no documented direct abuse history as a platform. Video players can be embedded with malicious advertising but Flowplayer itself is not a known abuse vector.

FlowTrack.history: FlowTrack is a US-based all-in-one CRM and sales automation platform positioning itself as a replacement for 14+ separate tools. It provides sales pipeline management, SMS automation, email marketing, appointment calendars, a phone system, and 200+ integrations with platforms like Salesforce, HubSpot, and Slack. The platform targets small to midsize businesses seeking to consolidate their marketing and sales stack.
FlowTrack.summary_abuse: No specific abuse or security incidents identified. FlowTrack is a CRM and marketing automation platform. The SMS and email marketing features could potentially be misused for spam campaigns if a vendor lacks adequate controls. No documented abuse incidents found. Limited public information available.

Fluid Framework.history: Fluid Framework was developed by Microsoft and open-sourced in December 2020. It powers collaborative features in Microsoft 365, Teams, Loop, and SharePoint Embedded. Fluid Framework 2.0 reached production readiness in 2024. The Azure Fluid Relay provides a managed cloud service backend for the framework.
Fluid Framework.summary_abuse: Fluid Framework is a Microsoft open-source library for building real-time collaborative apps with no documented abuse surface. As a developer library backed by Microsoft infrastructure, it has no consumer-facing exposure that would attract abuse.

Fluid Player.history: Fluid Player is an open source VAST and VPAID compliant HTML5 video player originally created and released by ExoClick, an ad network, in November 2016. The player was designed to support video advertising standards. It is MIT licensed and available on GitHub under the fluid-player organization. The player is also backed by EXADS, ExoClick's ad server technology. It supports VAST tags from any video ad server.
Fluid Player.summary_abuse: Fluid Player was created by ExoClick, an adult and mainstream ad network, and is primarily designed for video advertising integration (VAST/VPAID). This origin means the player is often embedded on ad-heavy or adult content sites. While the MIT-licensed code itself is clean, its association with ad networks and adult content distribution platforms raises moderate abuse signal. No security vulnerabilities in the library itself have been widely documented.

Fluro.history: Fluro is an Australian church management software platform providing tools for communication, volunteer management, child check-in, groups, email, rostering, planning, contact management, and reporting. The platform targets churches and faith-based organizations seeking to build deeper community relationships. Starting price is $49/month. Available on Android and iOS with webinar and live support.
Fluro.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Fluro is a legitimate church management SaaS targeting faith-based organizations in Australia. Handles congregation member data and child check-in information. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Flurry Analytics.history: Flurry was founded in San Francisco in 2005 as a mobile analytics platform. It became one of the largest mobile data companies, tracking behavior across 542,000+ apps. Yahoo acquired Flurry in 2014 for over $200M. Flurry collected extensive behavioral data across hundreds of thousands of mobile apps, raising significant privacy concerns. Yahoo announced the sunset of Flurry Analytics in March 2024.
Flurry Analytics.summary_abuse: Flurry is a cross-app mobile analytics tracker that collected behavioral data across 542,000+ apps, building detailed user profiles without explicit per-app consent. Privacy advocates and researchers have highlighted Flurry as a significant data harvesting concern given its embedded tracking in apps across many categories. Yahoo's deprecation of Flurry in 2024 reduces but does not eliminate the risk from existing SDK deployments in older apps.

Flutter.history: Flutter was started at Google in 2015 under the codename Sky and first demonstrated at the Dart Developer Summit. Flutter 1.0 was released in December 2018. Flutter 2.0 in March 2021 expanded support to web, Windows, macOS, and Linux. The framework is used by Google internally (Google Pay, Google Earth) and by companies like ByteDance and Alibaba. It became one of the most popular cross-platform mobile frameworks.
Flutter.summary_abuse: Flutter is a foundational cross-platform development framework with no abuse surface of its own. It is a server-side and client-side development tool with no data collection targeting end users. No abuse patterns documented.

FluxBB.history: FluxBB was forked from PunBB around 2008 when its development team sought a different direction for the project. It is a lightweight, fast PHP forum application under the GPL license. Flarum, a more modern forum software, was later created as a spiritual successor to FluxBB. Development of FluxBB has been largely dormant for several years.
FluxBB.summary_abuse: FluxBB is a dormant, self-hosted forum application with no centralized abuse surface. Individual FluxBB instances could be exploited for forum spam, but the platform itself is not a notable abuse vector.

Fly.io.history: Fly.io was founded in 2017 as a cloud application hosting platform that runs containers as hardware-virtualized VMs in 35+ global regions. The company positions itself as an alternative to Heroku and Vercel with more infrastructure control. It is VC-backed and has grown significantly in the developer community.
Fly.io.summary_abuse: Fly.io is a cloud hosting platform with a free tier that can be used to host arbitrary applications. Like other PaaS providers, it may be used to host malicious services, proxy servers, or C2 infrastructure, which is common for any developer cloud with free tiers. The company has an AUP and abuse reporting process.

Flybook.history: The Flybook was founded in 2006 by outdoor guides and is one of the earliest cloud-based reservation management platforms for the outdoor adventure industry. Based in the Pacific Northwest US (Oregon/Portland area), the platform serves zipline parks, rafting outfitters, glamping resorts, kayak rentals, guided tours, and multi-day adventure operations. Features include reservations, rentals, Smart Tickets, POS, lodging management, and 100+ integrations. 7-days-per-week US-based support.
Flybook.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. The Flybook is a legitimate vertical SaaS for outdoor recreation operators with a long track record since 2006. Handles booking and payment data for adventure tourism businesses. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Flying Analytics.history: Flying Analytics is a free WordPress plugin created by Gijo Varghese under the WP Speed Matters brand, released around 2021. It self-hosts Google Analytics v4 to improve page speed and privacy by eliminating the third-party GA script. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Flying Analytics.summary_abuse: Flying Analytics is a lightweight WordPress plugin with no notable abuse signal. It runs entirely on the site owner's own server, has no hosted service component, and no documented history of abuse.

Flying Images.history: Flying Images is a free open source WordPress plugin for high-performance lazy loading of images. It uses native browser lazy loading when available and falls back to JavaScript otherwise. The plugin integrates with Statically CDN for automatic image delivery and supports WebP conversion. It is available on WordPress.org and has received positive reviews for outperforming paid alternatives in Lighthouse/PageSpeed scores.
Flying Images.summary_abuse: No abuse or security concerns identified. Flying Images is a simple WordPress performance plugin with no tracking, no external data collection beyond optional CDN usage, and no monetization. Available free on WordPress.org under GPL.

Flying Pages.history: Flying Pages is a free WordPress plugin created by Gijo Varghese of WP Speed Matters, first released around 2019. It preloads pages intelligently when links enter the viewport, using a 1 KB script with built-in queue throttling to avoid server overload. The project is open source on GitHub with no notable ownership changes.
Flying Pages.summary_abuse: Flying Pages is a server-side WordPress plugin with no hosted service component and no documented abuse history. It runs entirely within the user's own WordPress installation.

FlyingPress.history: FlyingPress is a commercial WordPress performance plugin created by Gijo Varghese, the developer behind WP Speed Matters and the free Flying Pages and Flying Analytics plugins. First released around 2020, it offers caching, image optimization, lazy loading, and other performance features as a paid subscription plugin. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
FlyingPress.summary_abuse: FlyingPress is a WordPress plugin installed on the site owner's own server with no hosted service component or user-facing surface. No documented abuse patterns have been reported.

Flyspray.history: Flyspray is an open source lightweight PHP web-based bug tracking system originally developed for the Psi XMPP client project. Released around 2004 under LGPL 2.1, it supports MySQL and PostgreSQL, and offers multi-project support, email/RSS/XMPP notifications, file attachments, voting, and ticket dependencies. Used by PHP.net and listed alongside Bugzilla as a major open source bug tracker. Hosted on GitHub under the flyspray organization.
Flyspray.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns identified. Flyspray is a self-hosted open source bug tracker with no cloud component. It handles internal project data only. No tracking or data collection. No security incidents documented.

Flyvi.history: Flyvi is a Russian AI-powered graphic design platform launched around 2022, positioned as a Canva alternative for Russian-speaking users after Canva became unavailable in Russia. The platform provides 5,000+ free templates and 30,000+ premium templates, AI image tools (background removal, object replacement, image enhancement, WebP conversion), and design elements for social media, marketing, and business content.
Flyvi.summary_abuse: Flyvi is a Russian-based design platform primarily serving Russian-speaking users. Russian jurisdiction raises data sovereignty concerns. The platform positions itself as a Canva replacement following sanctions, making its user base partly comprised of users in sanctioned jurisdictions. No specific abuse incidents documented, but Russian ownership and target market creates data governance considerations.

Flywheel.history: Flywheel was founded in 2012 in Omaha, Nebraska as a managed WordPress hosting platform targeting designers and creative agencies. The company built workflow tools for agencies including billing transfer and collaboration features. In 2019, WP Engine acquired Flywheel, integrating it into its portfolio of managed WordPress hosting products. The platform serves over 100,000 customers.
Flywheel.summary_abuse: Flywheel is a managed WordPress hosting platform with a curated customer base of designers and agencies. As a paid managed hosting service, it has lower abuse risk than free hosting platforms. No notable abuse incidents documented.

Flyzoo.history: Flyzoo is a live chat and community platform for websites targeting communities, forums, shops, social networks, radio stations, and dating sites. It provides group chat, private messaging, live support routing, real-time visitor monitoring, and moderation tools. Available as plugins for WordPress, Joomla, and BuddyPress. Interface is available in 22 languages. 14-day free trial with no credit card required, subscriptions from $9.90/month.
Flyzoo.summary_abuse: Flyzoo is an embeddable chat platform. It targets dating sites, radio stations, and trading sites which increases potential for user harassment, spam, and financial scam risks within chat environments. The moderation tools including ban and word filtering are available but enforcement depends on site operators. No major platform-level abuse incidents documented.

FMG Suite.history: FMG Suite is a financial advisor marketing platform offering websites, email campaigns, and social media tools for RIAs and financial professionals. The company has been operating for over 10 years and has been consistently rated number one in customer satisfaction in T3/Inside Information surveys. No major ownership changes documented.
FMG Suite.summary_abuse: FMG Suite is a regulated-industry SaaS serving SEC-registered financial advisors with compliance-focused marketing tools. The platform archives all communications to meet regulatory requirements. No notable abuse or fraud incidents documented; the regulated customer base provides inherent AML and compliance controls.

FOCUS WebWall.history: FOCUS WebWall is a zero-overhead security layer for websites using Cloudflare, offered through the appsphere.app platform. It appears to be a small, independently developed security tool with limited public corporate history.
FOCUS WebWall.summary_abuse: FOCUS WebWall has no notable abuse signal. It is a Cloudflare-integrated security layer tool designed to protect websites from attacks, with no consumer-facing surface that would be attractive for phishing or fraud.

Fohr.history: Fohr was founded in 2013 in New York City and claims to be the first influencer marketing platform. The company manages influencer campaigns for category-leading brands including Sephora and has built a database of 300,000+ opt-in creators and 6,000 campaigns of data over 12 years.
Fohr.summary_abuse: Fohr is an influencer marketing platform with a curated network of opt-in creators. Influencer marketing platforms can be used for sponsored content that is not clearly disclosed, which is a regulatory concern (FTC compliance). No notable platform-level abuse incidents documented.

Foleon.history: Foleon was founded in 2013 in Amsterdam, Netherlands as an interactive content creation platform for businesses. The company has raised $15.9M from Connected Capital, Newion Investments, and VentureOut. It serves over 1,400 brands globally with a drag-and-drop editor for creating interactive digital documents.
Foleon.summary_abuse: Foleon is a B2B interactive content platform with no documented abuse history. The service is used to create branded digital documents and has no notable consumer-facing abuse surface.

Follow Up Boss.history: Follow Up Boss was founded in 2011 by Dan Corkill and Tom Markov as a real estate CRM platform. It integrates with 250+ lead sources and serves over 100,000 real estate agents. Zillow acquired Follow Up Boss in late 2023 for approximately $500 million, integrating it into its ecosystem while operating it as an independent brand.
Follow Up Boss.summary_abuse: Follow Up Boss is a real estate CRM with no documented abuse history. As a B2B CRM for real estate professionals backed by Zillow, it handles prospect and client contact data but has no notable consumer-facing abuse vectors.

Fomo.history: Fomo is a social proof SaaS platform that was originally called Notify and acquired by Ryan Kulp in 2016 for approximately $3,000. Kulp grew it to over $1 million ARR before stepping down as CEO in 2020. Fomo was subsequently acquired by Relay Commerce, a portfolio operator of e-commerce SaaS tools.
Fomo.summary_abuse: Fomo embeds social proof notifications on e-commerce sites, displaying activity such as recent purchases. Abuse potential is low as it is not a hosting or content platform. No notable documented abuse incidents.

Fondue.history: Fondue is a cashback promotions platform for Shopify brands built and run by Postscript, a leading SMS marketing platform serving 20,000+ Shopify stores. Fondue replaces traditional coupon codes with post-purchase cashback offers redeemable as gift cards or cash. The platform reports 30% average revenue boost with 70% of cashback orders coming in at full price. Used by 42+ brands. Integrates with Shopify checkout as a native extension.
Fondue.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Fondue is a Shopify-native cashback promotions tool backed by Postscript. The platform handles post-purchase reward redemption but processes no payment card data directly. No dark patterns or data misuse reported.

Font Awesome.history: Font Awesome was created by Dave Gandy and first released on February 2, 2012, originally as a free icon font for Bootstrap. The project gained massive adoption and Gandy co-founded Fort Awesome LLC with Travis Chase to commercialize it, going through Y Combinator in 2015 under the name Fonticons before rebranding to Fort Awesome in 2016. The project maintains a free open-source tier (Font Awesome Free, MIT-licensed icons) alongside a paid Pro tier.
Font Awesome.summary_abuse: Font Awesome is an icon library used on millions of legitimate websites with no notable abuse signal. It has no user content hosting surface and no documented use in malicious contexts.

Fontify.history: Fontify is a Shopify app for managing Google Fonts and custom fonts on Shopify stores with GDPR compliance. The app allows merchants to upload brand fonts in TTF, OTF, and WOFF formats, hosts fonts locally for speed, and provides visual element-by-element font customization without coding. The paid plan costs $3/month. The app has a strong reputation on the Shopify App Store for ease of use and GDPR-compliant font delivery.
Fontify.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Fontify is a simple Shopify font management app with minimal data collection. The GDPR compliance feature (local font hosting) actually reduces third-party data exposure compared to Google Fonts. No dark patterns or data misuse reported.

FontServer.history: FontServer is a free online font delivery network service based in Iran (.ir domain), officially started in 2019. The service delivers web fonts via CDN to websites, positioned as an alternative to Google Fonts for Persian/Iranian websites and beyond. Limited public information is available about the team size, ownership, or technical infrastructure.
FontServer.summary_abuse: FontServer is an Iranian font CDN service. Iranian-hosted infrastructure raises concerns about data sovereignty, reliability under international sanctions, and potential for traffic interception. As a CDN delivering fonts to websites globally, it has visibility into user IP addresses and browsing patterns. Limited transparency about operations. No formal security certifications or GDPR compliance. Not recommended for use on websites serving EU users.

Food-Ordering.co.uk.history: Food-Ordering.co.uk is a multi-lingual UK hospitality ordering platform supporting online ordering for delivery and takeout, in-store ordering (order at table, room service, self-service kiosks), telephone ordering with caller ID integration, and table booking. The hardware-agnostic platform serves restaurants, cafes, hotels, and other hospitality venues with a comprehensive omnichannel ordering solution.
Food-Ordering.co.uk.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. Food-Ordering.co.uk is a small UK hospitality SaaS with limited public profile. As a UK company it operates under UK GDPR. Handles customer food order data and payments for restaurants. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

FoodBooking.history: FoodBooking is a virtual food court platform built on top of GloriaFood's restaurant ordering system. It presents a curated list of restaurants using GloriaFood's online ordering system to end consumers, allowing customers to discover and order from multiple restaurants. GloriaFood is a Romanian company that provides free online ordering software for restaurants globally.
FoodBooking.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents identified. FoodBooking is a GloriaFood-powered restaurant discovery and ordering platform. Handles food ordering and customer contact data. As a European company it operates under GDPR. No data misuse or deceptive practices reported.

Foodomaa.history: Foodomaa is a multi-restaurant food ordering and restaurant management SaaS platform founded around 2019 in Bangladesh. It offers white-label solutions for restaurant owners.
Foodomaa.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk; a niche food-ordering platform with limited consumer-facing exposure. No documented abuse patterns.

FooPlugins FooGallery.history: FooGallery is a WordPress gallery plugin created and maintained by Brad and Tam Vincent through FooPlugins, a team that has been building WordPress plugins for photographers and developers since 2004. The plugin is available free on WordPress.org with a Pro version offering additional features including WooCommerce integration for selling photos, video gallery support (YouTube/Vimeo), and advanced filtering. It is Gutenberg-compatible and optimized for Core Web Vitals.
FooPlugins FooGallery.summary_abuse: No abuse or security concerns identified. FooGallery is a well-maintained open source WordPress gallery plugin by a reputable team with over 20 years in the WordPress community. No security incidents or data collection concerns documented.

FooTable.history: FooTable is an open source jQuery plugin for making HTML tables responsive, created by FooPlugins (Brad Vincent). Released around 2012 under MIT license, it hides table columns at different breakpoints and makes rows expandable to reveal hidden data. It includes add-ons for sorting, filtering, and pagination. Available via npm and on GitHub under fooplugins/FooTable. Development has slowed in recent years and the project appears dormant.
FooTable.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. FooTable is a simple client-side jQuery table library. MIT licensed with no tracking or data collection. No security incidents documented.

Foratable.history: Foratable is a restaurant reservation and table management SaaS founded around 2014, headquartered in Germany. It serves restaurants across Europe.
Foratable.summary_abuse: Minimal abuse risk. A reservation management tool with no significant documented abuse patterns.

Forento.history: Forento is a Swedish e-learning and digital product platform founded around 2020, allowing creators to sell online courses, memberships, and downloads.
Forento.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A course platform with limited consumer-facing surface. No notable abuse patterns documented.

Forethought AI.history: Forethought AI was founded in 2017 in San Francisco, providing AI-powered customer support automation. The company raised significant VC funding and developed its Solve product for support ticket deflection.
Forethought AI.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An enterprise AI support platform. No documented abuse patterns.

Forethought Solve.history: Forethought Solve is the customer support AI product by Forethought AI (founded 2017, San Francisco). It uses AI to deflect and resolve support tickets automatically.
Forethought Solve.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. Enterprise AI support automation with no notable abuse patterns.

ForeUP.history: ForeUP is a US-based golf management SaaS platform founded in 2013, providing tee sheet, POS, and marketing tools to golf courses. It has received venture funding.
ForeUP.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A vertical SaaS for golf management with no notable abuse patterns.

Forgejo.history: Forgejo is an open-source, community-driven fork of Gitea, created in 2022 when concerns arose over the commercialization of Gitea. It is stewarded by Codeberg e.V., a German nonprofit.
Forgejo.summary_abuse: Very low abuse risk. A self-hosted git forge with no notable consumer-facing abuse surface.

Forie.history: Forie is a B2B marketplace software platform for connecting suppliers and buyers in business-to-business transactions. Minimal corporate history is publicly available; the company appears to be a small, recently launched independent startup.
Forie.summary_abuse: Forie has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B marketplace platform serving business transactions between verified suppliers and buyers, with no anonymous consumer-facing features that would attract fraud campaigns.

Fork Awesome.history: Fork Awesome is a fork of Font Awesome created in 2018 by the community after concerns about the direction of Font Awesome. The project has been relatively dormant in recent years.
Fork Awesome.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. An icon font library with no consumer-facing surface.

Fork CMS.history: Fork CMS is an open-source content management system developed in Belgium, first released in 2010. It focuses on multilingual websites and SEO.
Fork CMS.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An open-source CMS; minimal consumer-facing abuse surface.

Form.io.history: Form.io is a US-based form and API platform founded in 2015. It provides drag-and-drop form building with API generation, targeting enterprise developers. The company has received venture funding.
Form.io.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A developer-focused form platform. Phishing forms can occasionally be built on any form tool, but Form.io has reasonable abuse controls.

Form.taxi.history: Form.taxi is a German form backend service founded around 2019, providing form submission handling for static sites without requiring a server.
Form.taxi.summary_abuse: Moderate abuse risk for a form backend. Spam submissions are a common vector on any form service; Form.taxi includes basic spam protection.

Formalize CSS.history: Formalize CSS is an open-source CSS library for consistent cross-browser form styling, created around 2011. The project has been largely dormant for many years.
Formalize CSS.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. A front-end CSS library with no consumer-facing surface.

Formaloo.history: Formaloo is an Iranian no-code form and app builder founded in 2019, offering data collection and CRM features. It serves markets in the Middle East and globally.
Formaloo.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. A form platform that could be used for phishing, but primarily serves legitimate business users.

FormAssembly.history: FormAssembly is a US-based enterprise form builder founded in 2006. It focuses on compliance-heavy industries including healthcare and government. The company received VC funding.
FormAssembly.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. FormAssembly is used in regulated industries; it has security certifications reducing abuse likelihood. Phishing forms built on platforms like this occasionally appear.

Format.history: Format is a Canadian website builder for photographers and creative professionals, founded around 2012. It provides portfolio hosting and client proofing tools.
Format.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A portfolio hosting platform for creatives with minimal documented abuse.

FormBold.history: FormBold is a form backend service founded around 2021, allowing developers to handle form submissions from static sites without a backend.
FormBold.summary_abuse: Moderate abuse risk for a form endpoint service. Spam and phishing forms are common misuse patterns for such services.

FormBucket.history: FormBucket is a form backend service founded around 2017 providing form submission storage and notification for static websites.
FormBucket.summary_abuse: Moderate abuse risk typical for form backend services. Spam submissions are the primary concern.

Formcake.history: Formcake is a form backend service for static sites founded around 2019, handling form submissions and notifications.
Formcake.summary_abuse: Moderate abuse risk as a form endpoint. Spam is a common misuse pattern for form backend services.

Formcan.history: Formcan is an online form builder SaaS launched around 2021 offering creation, customization, and deployment of forms for data collection. It is a small independent startup with limited public corporate history.
Formcan.summary_abuse: Formcan is a form builder platform that allows creation of data collection forms. Online form builders can be misused to create phishing credential-harvesting pages. Formcan's free tier and self-service signup make it available for unauthenticated use. No documented specific abuse incidents are known for this particular vendor.

FormDr.history: FormDr is a US-based HIPAA-compliant online form builder for healthcare providers, founded around 2013. It specializes in patient intake forms.
FormDr.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. Focused on healthcare compliance forms with HIPAA support. Limited consumer-facing abuse surface.

Formester.history: Formester is an Indian form builder SaaS founded around 2022, offering no-code form creation and submissions management.
Formester.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk typical for form platforms. Spam and phishing forms are potential misuse patterns.

Formidable Form.history: Formidable Forms is a WordPress form builder plugin founded around 2012. It has grown to be one of the more feature-rich WordPress form plugins, supporting complex forms and calculations.
Formidable Form.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. A WordPress plugin; phishing forms are occasionally built with any form plugin but Formidable has reasonable spam controls.

Formitable.history: Formitable was founded in 2017 in Amsterdam, Netherlands, as a restaurant reservation management system. In 2022 it raised €8M led by Adriaan Mol (co-founder of Mollie) and formed a partnership with Airbnb. In 2024, Formitable merged with Paris-based Zenchef to create a combined European restaurant tech platform serving over 3,000 restaurants across 15 countries.
Formitable.summary_abuse: Formitable has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B restaurant reservation and management platform serving verified restaurant businesses. No consumer-facing content hosting or anonymous signup is present.

Formless.history: Formless is an AI-powered conversational form platform founded in 2022, enabling users to replace static forms with dynamic AI chat interfaces.
Formless.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An AI form platform; limited consumer-facing abuse surface documented.

Formli.history: Formli is a web-based form builder service offering survey, feedback, and registration forms with a freemium pricing model. It is a small, recently launched independent product with limited public corporate history.
Formli.summary_abuse: Formli is a form builder with a free tier and self-service signup. Online form builders with anonymous access can be misused to create phishing credential-harvesting pages. No specific documented abuse incidents are known for this vendor.

Formrun.history: Formrun is a Japanese form builder and CRM platform founded in 2017, with strong adoption in Japan. It provides form creation, data management, and team collaboration features.
Formrun.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A Japanese business form platform with limited documented abuse.

Formsite.history: Formsite was launched in 1998 as one of the earliest online form builders, growing organically to over 11,500 customers and $6.5M ARR by 2020. In December 2023, Formstack acquired Formsite to expand its form builder portfolio.
Formsite.summary_abuse: Formsite is a form hosting service that collects and stores user submissions, which can be abused for phishing credential collection. However, Formsite has robust security features and HTTPS enforcement. Abuse is occasional and consistent with the broader form-hosting category rather than being a specific target.

Formsort.history: Formsort is a US-based form builder for compliance-sensitive industries like fintech, founded around 2019. It has raised VC funding and focuses on conversion-optimized forms.
Formsort.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. Primarily serves fintech and compliance use cases with enterprise controls.

Formstack.history: Formstack was founded by Ade Olonoh on February 28, 2006 in Fishers, Indiana as a drag-and-drop form builder originally called FormSpring. The social Q&A site formspring.me was spun out separately in 2010 and FormSpring.com became Formstack.com. PSG led a leveraged buyout in 2018. In November 2021, Formstack raised $425M from Silversmith and PSG. In December 2023, Formstack acquired Formsite. The company rebranded as Intellistack in June 2025.
Formstack.summary_abuse: Formstack hosts web forms on behalf of organizations. Like other form platforms, forms can be abused to collect credentials via phishing pages. Formstack has SOC 2 Type 2 and HIPAA compliance programs and publishes a security policy, indicating reasonable anti-abuse posture. No major documented abuse incidents were found.

Formstone.history: Formstone is an open-source jQuery plugin library for form elements founded around 2013. The project has been dormant for several years.
Formstone.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. A front-end jQuery library with no consumer-facing surface.

Formtoro.history: Formtoro is a zero-party data collection platform that converts e-commerce popup signups into marketing insights through quizzes and post-purchase surveys. It appears to be a small independent startup launched around 2021 targeting DTC e-commerce brands.
Formtoro.summary_abuse: Formtoro has no notable abuse signal. It is a marketing data collection tool for e-commerce merchants with no consumer-facing anonymous access. The platform collects zero-party data through branded merchant popups rather than operating as a standalone site.

FormWise.history: FormWise is an AI tool builder platform launched around 2023 that enables users to create white-label AI tools powered by ChatGPT prompts without coding. It is positioned as part of the post-GPT-4 wave of no-code AI wrapper products.
FormWise.summary_abuse: FormWise is a ChatGPT prompt wrapper that allows creation of white-label AI tools. As with all AI tool builder platforms, it could theoretically be used to generate convincing phishing content at scale, though no specific documented abuse is associated with it. The platform's white-label nature means end users may encounter AI tools built on it without knowing the underlying provider.

ForoshGostar.history: ForoshGostar is an Iranian e-commerce platform and website builder serving the domestic Iranian market. The platform is used almost exclusively within Iran (99% of detected instances) and powers online stores across categories including health and beauty, electronics, and automotive. The product also operates a Forever Iran shopping app for Iranian consumers.
ForoshGostar.summary_abuse: ForoshGostar is an Iranian e-commerce platform operating primarily within Iran. Iranian-based platforms operate outside GDPR and Western regulatory frameworks for data protection and consumer protection. No specific documented fraud or phishing incidents are associated with this platform, but limited international oversight applies.

Forsant.history: Forsant is a Russian email and marketing automation platform founded around 2018, serving Eastern European markets.
Forsant.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. Email marketing platforms can be misused for spam, though Forsant serves primarily legitimate businesses.

Forte.history: Forte is a US payment processing platform founded in 2001, acquired by ACI Worldwide. It serves small and mid-size businesses with payment gateway and merchant services.
Forte.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A payment processor with compliance requirements that limit abuse exposure.

Forter.history: Forter is a fraud prevention platform founded in 2013 in Tel Aviv/New York. It uses machine learning for real-time transaction fraud decisions. The company has raised over $500M in VC funding.
Forter.summary_abuse: Very low abuse risk. Forter is itself a fraud prevention service; it processes payment data under strict compliance controls.

Fortinet FortiGate.history: FortiGate is Fortinet's flagship firewall and network security product, launched in 2002. Fortinet was founded in 2000 in Sunnyvale, CA and went public in 2009. FortiGate dominates the enterprise firewall market.
Fortinet FortiGate.summary_abuse: No abuse risk from FortiGate itself. It is a security appliance used by enterprises to prevent attacks, not a consumer-facing service.

Fortune3.history: Fortune3 is a US-based e-commerce platform founded around 2006, providing online store creation and management tools for small businesses.
Fortune3.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A small e-commerce platform with no notable documented abuse patterns.

Forumbee.history: Forumbee was founded in 2012 by Chris Greener in the San Francisco Bay Area as a hosted community platform for customer and employee Q&A, discussion forums, and idea generation. The company has remained small and bootstrapped, serving SMB and mid-market clients needing branded community spaces.
Forumbee.summary_abuse: Forumbee has no notable abuse signal. It is a B2B community forum platform where vendor clients set up private or semi-private communities for their customers and employees. The platform requires business clients and does not offer fully anonymous public forum hosting at consumer scale.

Foswiki.history: Foswiki was launched in October 2008 as a fork of TWiki, after a governance and commercialization dispute between TWIKI.NET and the broader TWiki community. Nearly all key TWiki contributors migrated to the new project. Version 1.0.0 was released on January 9, 2009. The project is governed by the Foswiki Association e.V., a volunteer-run non-profit foundation.
Foswiki.summary_abuse: Foswiki is a self-hosted open source wiki with no hosted service component and no documented abuse signal as a software project.

Four.history: Four (formerly Splitit) or 'Four by Splitit' is a buy-now-pay-later payment solution. Founded around 2018, providing installment payment options to merchants.
Four.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. BNPL platforms can be used for payment fraud but have card validation requirements.

Foursixty.history: Foursixty is a shoppable UGC and social gallery SaaS platform for e-commerce brands, integrating Instagram, TikTok, and other social media content into shoppable product galleries on Shopify and other e-commerce platforms. Founded around 2014, it is trusted by over 2,000 brands. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Foursixty.summary_abuse: Foursixty is an e-commerce gallery tool that aggregates social media content from verified brand accounts. No notable abuse signal has been documented for this service.

Fourthwall.history: Fourthwall is a US creator commerce platform founded in 2020, allowing content creators to sell merchandise and memberships. It raised VC funding and grew rapidly with YouTubers and streamers.
Fourthwall.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. A creator merch platform; counterfeiting and IP infringement are concerns, but Fourthwall has policies against these.

FoxPush.history: FoxPush is a web push notification advertising network founded around 2015 in Lebanon. It operates a publisher monetization network using web push notifications.
FoxPush.summary_abuse: Moderate-high abuse risk. Push notification ad networks are commonly used for adware and unwanted notification spam. FoxPush has been associated with aggressive push notification practices.

Foxy.io.history: Foxy.io, originally FoxyCart, was founded in 2007 by Luke Stokes as a lightweight hosted cart and payment solution for web developers who wanted e-commerce without a full platform lock-in. The company operates as an independent bootstrapped business based in Canada, with a distributed team of developers. It reached $4.8M in annual revenue by 2023 with approximately 1,000 merchant clients.
Foxy.io.summary_abuse: Foxy.io is an e-commerce cart and payment processing platform with standard merchant fraud risks. Like all payment page providers, it could be misused by fraudulent merchants to collect payments on fake storefronts, though Foxy requires business account setup and payment gateway integration. No specific documented abuse campaigns are associated with this platform.

Framer Motion.history: Framer Motion is an open-source React animation library released in 2018 by Framer, a Swedish design tools company. It has become one of the most popular animation libraries for React.
Framer Motion.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. A JavaScript animation library with no consumer-facing surface.

Framer Sites.history: Framer Sites is the website publishing platform by Framer, a Swedish design and prototyping company founded in 2013. The sites product launched around 2019 and has grown rapidly.
Framer Sites.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. Like any website builder, phishing pages can be created but Framer has abuse prevention policies.

Frames.history: Frames is a WordPress design and wireframing plugin that provides a component library for building custom websites within WordPress. It is distributed as a commercial product via getframes.io with limited public corporate history.
Frames.summary_abuse: Frames has no notable abuse signal. It is a WordPress design plugin distributed through a commercial product channel with no consumer-facing anonymous features.

Framework7.history: Framework7 is an open-source mobile UI framework for building iOS and Android apps with HTML/CSS/JS, created by Vladimir Kharlampidi in 2014. It is widely used for hybrid mobile app development.
Framework7.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. A mobile UI framework library with no consumer-facing surface.

framework7.history: Framework7 is an open-source mobile HTML framework created around 2014 for building iOS and Android-style web and hybrid mobile apps. It is maintained independently.
framework7.summary_abuse: Framework7 is a mobile UI framework with no notable abuse signal. It is a frontend development tool with no server-side or content-hosting surface.

France Express.history: France Express was established in March 1971 as a joint venture between Sotrab and SFTC in Brittany, France, becoming one of the early express courier networks in the country. The Calberson group acquired a stake in 1977, and in 1995 Calberson joined the Geodis group, bringing France Express under the Geodis umbrella as a subsidiary of SNCF, France's state railway operator. The network grew to 110+ depots and over 3,500 employees across metropolitan France and Corsica.
France Express.summary_abuse: France Express has no notable abuse signal as a technology. It is a French parcel courier tracking integration, and the physical delivery network itself is a legitimate subsidiary of SNCF through Geodis. Shipping carrier integrations are used legitimately to track packages.

Frappe.history: Frappe was founded in 2008 by Rushabh Mehta in Mumbai, India. Mehta originally built the framework to address a failed ERP implementation at his family's furniture business. The underlying web framework (Frappe Framework) powers ERPNext, which was open sourced starting in 2010 and positioned as open source ERP from 2012. The company is notably profitable and independent with no external investors.
Frappe.summary_abuse: Frappe is a Python web framework and ERP platform used primarily in enterprise environments. It is self-hosted software with no notable abuse signal as a software project.

Frase.history: Frase is a US-based AI content creation and SEO optimization platform founded in 2018. It helps content teams research, write, and optimize content. The company has raised VC funding.
Frase.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An AI writing tool; potential for generating spam content but primarily used by legitimate marketers.

Fraud Blocker.history: Fraud Blocker is a US click fraud detection and prevention service for Google Ads, founded around 2019. It protects advertisers from invalid traffic.
Fraud Blocker.summary_abuse: Very low abuse risk. Fraud Blocker is itself an anti-fraud service for advertisers.

FraudLabs Pro.history: FraudLabs Pro is a Malaysian fraud detection service for e-commerce founded in 2009. It provides fraud screening for online transactions using machine learning.
FraudLabs Pro.summary_abuse: Very low abuse risk. FraudLabs Pro is an anti-fraud platform protecting merchants from payment fraud.

FreakOut.history: FreakOut is a Japanese digital advertising and DSP platform founded in 2010. The company went public and operates programmatic advertising technology across Asia.
FreakOut.summary_abuse: Moderate abuse risk. Ad tech platforms are frequently used for malvertising. FreakOut as a DSP may serve ads that include trackers or lead to low-quality content.

Frederick.history: Frederick is a marketing automation platform for service businesses, acquired by Demandforce. It helps salons, spas, and service businesses with automated marketing.
Frederick.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A service-business marketing tool with limited consumer-facing abuse surface.

FreeBSD.history: FreeBSD is a free and open-source Unix-like operating system descending from BSD, released in 1993. It is developed by the FreeBSD Foundation and a large community. It powers PlayStation, Netflix, and many embedded systems.
FreeBSD.summary_abuse: No abuse risk from FreeBSD itself. It is a foundational operating system used in critical infrastructure. Some attackers run servers on FreeBSD but this is not a distinguishing characteristic.

Freedom Farmers.history: Freedom Farmers is a US-based online learning and community platform for market farmers and food producers, founded around 2019.
Freedom Farmers.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A niche educational community platform for farmers with limited documented abuse.

FreedomKit.history: FreedomKit is an AI-powered all-in-one online business automation platform launched around 2023, targeting solopreneurs and small businesses with marketing automation, CRM, and funnel-building capabilities. Minimal corporate history is publicly available.
FreedomKit.summary_abuse: FreedomKit has no notable documented abuse signal. It is a marketing automation and funnel-building platform. Like similar tools, it could be used to build scam landing pages, but no specific abuse incidents are documented.

Freemius.history: Freemius is an Israeli software monetization platform founded in 2015, providing licensing, payments, and analytics for WordPress plugin and theme developers.
Freemius.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A software licensing platform for developers. No significant documented abuse patterns.

Freespee.history: Freespee is a Swedish call analytics and conversation intelligence platform founded in 2009. It provides phone call tracking for marketing attribution. The company raised VC funding.
Freespee.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A call tracking analytics platform. Some privacy concerns with call tracking technology but no documented abuse.

FreeTextBox.history: FreeTextBox was an open-source HTML rich text editor for ASP.NET, first released around 2003. The project has been abandoned for over a decade.
FreeTextBox.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. An abandoned web text editor library with no consumer-facing surface.

freewebstore.history: freewebstore is a UK-based platform allowing users to create free e-commerce stores; it has operated since the late 2000s catering to small businesses and individuals wanting a zero-cost entry point to online selling.
freewebstore.summary_abuse: freewebstore offers free store creation with no payment required upfront, which creates some risk of fake storefronts and scam shops being set up; however, its profile is relatively low compared to major free-tier builders and no specific large-scale abuse incidents are documented.

Frequenceo.history: Frequenceo is a fixed-rate bulk mail service offered by La Poste, the French state-owned postal service established in the early 20th century. It is a business mailing product for companies sending large volumes of mail at fixed rates in France.
Frequenceo.summary_abuse: Frequenceo has no notable abuse signal. It is a physical bulk postage service from La Poste, the French national postal operator, with standard regulatory compliance for business customers.

Frequently Bought Together.history: Frequently Bought Together is a Shopify app providing product recommendation and upsell features based on Amazon-style purchase patterns, developed around 2017.
Frequently Bought Together.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A Shopify upsell plugin with no direct consumer-facing abuse surface.

Fresco.history: Fresco is a collaborative visual planning and whiteboarding platform founded around 2019, offering infinite canvas tools for teams.
Fresco.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A collaborative workspace tool with no notable documented abuse.

Fresh.history: Fresh is an open-source web framework for Deno created by Deno Land Inc in 2022. It uses islands architecture for optimal performance and ships zero JS by default.
Fresh.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. A server-side web framework with no consumer-facing surface.

Fresh Relevance.history: Fresh Relevance is a UK-based real-time personalization and marketing platform founded in 2012. It helps e-commerce brands with personalized email and web content. The company raised VC funding.
Fresh Relevance.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. Real-time personalization uses tracking technologies; vendor has GDPR compliance processes.

Fresha.history: Fresha (formerly Shedul) is a UK-based booking and POS platform for beauty and wellness businesses, founded in 2015. It pivoted to a free model and grew rapidly. The company raised over $100M in VC funding.
Fresha.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A booking platform for salons and spas with limited documented abuse patterns.

Freshchat.history: Freshchat is the live chat and messaging product from Freshworks, launched around 2016 as part of Freshworks' expansion beyond its original Freshdesk help desk product. Freshworks was founded in 2010 in Chennai, India by Girish Mathrubootham and Shan Krishnasamy. The company went public on NASDAQ in September 2021 as the first Indian SaaS company listed there.
Freshchat.summary_abuse: Freshchat is an enterprise live chat platform by the publicly listed Freshworks. No notable abuse signal has been documented. Freshworks publishes SOC 2 Type 2 and ISO 27001 certifications and a responsible disclosure policy.

Fresho.history: Fresho is an Australian food wholesale ordering and management platform founded in 2014, connecting food producers and wholesalers with restaurants and hospitality businesses.
Fresho.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A B2B food wholesale platform with limited consumer-facing exposure.

Freshop.history: Freshop is a US grocery e-commerce platform founded around 2013, providing online shopping and fulfillment solutions for independent grocery retailers.
Freshop.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A grocery e-commerce platform with standard retail security practices.

Freshteam.history: Freshteam is the HR software product of Freshworks, launched in 2017 as part of the company's expansion beyond customer support tools. Freshworks was founded in 2010 by Girish Mathrubootham and Shan Krishnasamy in Chennai, India, and went public on NASDAQ in September 2021.
Freshteam.summary_abuse: Freshteam is an HR and recruiting platform by the publicly traded Freshworks. It handles sensitive employee and candidate data, but no notable abuse patterns or security incidents have been documented for this specific product.

Freshworks CRM.history: Freshworks CRM (now Freshsales) is a CRM product by Freshworks, an Indian SaaS company founded in 2010 that went public on NASDAQ in 2021. The CRM product launched around 2016.
Freshworks CRM.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A publicly traded enterprise SaaS company with robust compliance and security practices.

Friendbuy.history: Friendbuy is a US referral marketing and loyalty platform founded in 2010, helping e-commerce brands launch refer-a-friend programs. The company has raised VC funding.
Friendbuy.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. Referral programs can be gamed with fake accounts, but Friendbuy provides fraud detection for referral abuse.

Friendly Analytics.history: Friendly Analytics is an Austrian privacy-focused web analytics platform based on Matomo, founded around 2020. It offers GDPR-compliant analytics as a hosted service.
Friendly Analytics.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. A privacy-focused analytics tool designed to minimize data collection.

Friendly Captcha.history: Friendly Captcha is a German privacy-first CAPTCHA service founded around 2020.
