It uses a proof-of-work mechanism instead of invasive behavioral tracking, making it GDPR-compliant by design. The service was created as a privacy-respecting alternative to Google reCAPTCHA and has EU-only server endpoints. No notable ownership changes or controversies.
Friendly Captcha.summary_abuse: Friendly Captcha is an anti-bot service, not a content hosting platform. It does not store personal data or enable user-generated content and has no documented abuse signal.

Frill.history: Founded in 2019 in Australia as a customer feedback roadmap and announcements SaaS tool; no notable ownership changes or deprecation events since.
Frill.summary_abuse: Frill is a niche feedback management tool with no notable abuse signal. It does not offer free hosting of arbitrary content or anonymous signups.

Frisbie.history: Frisbie is a Russian-origin live chat widget service offering messenger buttons for Telegram, WhatsApp, and Viber; no notable ownership changes documented.
Frisbie.summary_abuse: Frisbie is a small niche chat widget primarily serving Russian-language markets. No notable abuse reports documented. The widget embeds messenger links which could theoretically redirect users, but abuse surface is minimal relative to legitimate use.

Frizbit.history: Frizbit was founded in Istanbul, Turkey in 2016 by Ilhan Adiyaman, Ata Gur, and Tevfik Ince, after working at Turk Telekom. Named among the top three startups at Startup Turkey 2017, the co-founders relocated to Barcelona in late 2016 and participated in the Startup Bootcamp IoT & Data Tech acceleration program. The company is incorporated in Delaware and maintains offices in Barcelona.
Frizbit.summary_abuse: Frizbit is a web push notification and marketing automation platform. Web push notification services can be abused to send spam or deceptive notifications; however, Frizbit targets legitimate e-commerce businesses and has no specific documented abuse incidents. The technology can be misused if site operators obtain push permission through dark patterns.

Froala Editor.history: Froala Editor was created around 2012 as a lightweight WYSIWYG HTML editor written in JavaScript, targeting developers who needed an embeddable rich-text editing component. Distributed via ComponentSource since 2020. The company introduced annual and perpetual licensing options in January 2021. No major acquisitions or ownership changes are documented.
Froala Editor.summary_abuse: Froala Editor is a client-side JavaScript library embedded in third-party websites for rich text editing. It has no notable abuse signal as it has no consumer-facing surface, no hosting capabilities, and no URL-shortening or file-hosting features. Abuse potential is limited to XSS if improperly sanitized by developers, which is a standard risk for all WYSIWYG editors rather than a Froala-specific issue. No documented abuse incidents or security research findings specific to Froala.

Froged.history: Founded in 2019 in Malaga, Spain; operates as an omnichannel customer communication and live chat SaaS platform with no notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
Froged.summary_abuse: Froged is a B2B customer communication platform with no notable abuse signal. It does not offer free hosting of arbitrary content or anonymous signups, and its primary surface is business-to-customer messaging.

Front AI.history: Front AI is an AI-powered customer communication tool, part of the Front platform ecosystem. Front was founded in 2013 in San Francisco.
Front AI.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An enterprise customer communication platform with no notable documented abuse.

Front Chat.history: Front Chat is a live chat feature within the Front customer communication platform, founded in 2013 in San Francisco. Front raised over $200M in VC funding.
Front Chat.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An enterprise customer communication tool with SOC 2 compliance.

Front-Commerce.history: Front-Commerce is a French headless e-commerce frontend framework founded in 2017, specializing in PWA storefronts for Magento, Wordpress, and other backends.
Front-Commerce.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A developer-focused headless commerce framework with no consumer-facing abuse surface.

Frontastic.history: Founded in 2017 in Germany as a composable frontend platform for headless commerce; acquired by commercetools in November 2021 and integrated as its frontend product.
Frontastic.summary_abuse: Frontastic is a B2B headless commerce frontend platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves enterprise and mid-market retailers and has no consumer-facing content hosting surface that would attract scammers.

Frontify.history: Frontify was founded in 2013 in St. Gallen, Switzerland by Roger Dudler with an initial angel round of CHF 50,000. The company raised an $8.3M Series A in 2018 led by Blossom Capital, a $22.3M Series B in 2020 led by EQT Ventures, and a $50M Series C in 2021 led by Revaia, bringing total funding to over $80M. The platform serves as a centralized brand management hub with no major acquisitions or rebrands.
Frontify.summary_abuse: Frontify is a B2B brand management SaaS platform used by enterprise marketing and design teams. It has no notable abuse signal as it requires business authentication and does not offer a public hosting or file-sharing surface accessible to anonymous users. No documented abuse incidents.

Frontity.history: Frontity is an open-source React framework for WordPress, created in Spain in 2018. Automattic acquired Frontity in 2022 and later deprecated the project.
Frontity.summary_abuse: No abuse risk. An abandoned open-source framework with no consumer-facing surface.

Frontlead.history: Frontlead is a German lead generation and quiz funnel builder founded around 2019, helping businesses generate leads through interactive calculators and forms.
Frontlead.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A lead generation form tool. Phishing forms could be created but the primary use case is legitimate lead generation.

FrontPage.history: Microsoft FrontPage was a web design and HTML editor sold by Microsoft from 1997 until it was discontinued in 2006. It was originally developed by Vermeer Technologies and acquired by Microsoft in 1996.
FrontPage.summary_abuse: Very low current abuse risk as a deprecated product. FrontPage was replaced by Microsoft Expression Web.

Frontstream.history: Frontstream is a US-based nonprofit fundraising and employee engagement platform founded around 2009. It provides online fundraising tools and corporate giving platforms.
Frontstream.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A nonprofit fundraising platform with standard payment compliance.

Froonze.history: Froonze was founded in 2021 as a Shopify app developer offering customer account enhancement tools including loyalty programs and wishlists; no notable acquisitions since.
Froonze.summary_abuse: Froonze is a Shopify app enhancing customer account pages with no notable abuse signal. Its functionality is limited to the merchant Shopify store context and does not expose consumer-facing content hosting.

Frosmo.history: Frosmo was founded in 2008 in Helsinki, Finland by Mikael Gummerus after he identified a market need for AI-driven personalization software following his experience founding a mobile gaming company. Co-founders include Henry Nyberg, Aki Salakka, and Markku Mantere. The company grew to serve ecommerce and iGaming sectors with offices in Poland, UK, Singapore, Spain and Sweden. No major acquisitions or rebrands are documented.
Frosmo.summary_abuse: Frosmo is a B2B personalization platform serving enterprises in ecommerce and iGaming. It has no notable abuse signal, as it is a backend analytics and personalization service without a consumer-facing signup or hosting surface. No documented abuse incidents.

FUDforum.history: FUDforum (Fast Uncompromising Discussion Forum) is an open-source PHP forum software originally created by Advanced Internet Designs Inc. It has been maintained by the community since around 2001. Version 3.2.0 was released in January 2025 adding PHP 8 support and XSS security fixes. The software is one of many legacy PHP forum systems alongside phpBB and SMF.
FUDforum.summary_abuse: FUDforum is a self-hosted PHP forum system with a small user base. Like any public forum software, it can be exploited for spam if not properly configured with CAPTCHAs and moderation. Several historical CVEs exist for XSS vulnerabilities. No organized abuse campaigns documented.

Fueled.history: Fueled is a US mobile app design and development agency founded in 2008 in New York. It builds custom iOS and Android apps for brands and enterprises.
Fueled.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A software development agency with no direct consumer-facing product.

FUGU.history: Project Fugu is a Google/Chromium initiative to bring native app capabilities to the web platform. The associated browser extension and tooling were created by Google engineers, primarily Thomas Steiner, to help developers detect which Fugu APIs are used by websites. The project began around 2019.
FUGU.summary_abuse: FUGU is a developer tool and browser extension for detecting web platform capabilities used by websites. As a read-only diagnostic tool, it has no abuse surface and is not associated with any harmful use.

Full On Sport.history: Founded in 2009 in Sherborne, United Kingdom as an online event registration platform for sports and fundraising events; no notable ownership changes since.
Full On Sport.summary_abuse: Full On Sport is a niche sports event registration platform with no notable abuse signal. It processes event entries for race directors and sports clubs and does not offer general-purpose content hosting.

Full Slate.history: Full Slate is a US-based appointment scheduling software founded in 2008, targeting small service businesses. It has been operating for over 15 years without major ownership changes.
Full Slate.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An appointment scheduling tool for small businesses with no notable abuse patterns.

Fullbay.history: Fullbay is a cloud-based shop management platform for heavy-duty commercial truck and trailer repair shops, founded around 2014 and headquartered in the United States. The company built a subscription SaaS targeting independent diesel repair shops and fleet operators. No major acquisitions or rebrands are documented.
Fullbay.summary_abuse: Fullbay is a niche B2B fleet maintenance software platform serving commercial repair shops. It has no notable abuse signal given its closed business-to-business nature, payment processing integrations, and industry-specific user base. No documented abuse incidents.

FullCalendar.history: FullCalendar is an open-source JavaScript calendar library that has been actively developed for over 10 years, originally created around 2010. The standard version is MIT-licensed and free, while a premium version with additional plugins is commercially licensed starting at $480. The project has over 120 contributors on GitHub and consistently ranks as one of the most popular JavaScript calendar components. No major ownership changes or forks.
FullCalendar.summary_abuse: FullCalendar is a frontend JavaScript library for rendering calendar widgets. It has no notable abuse signal as it is a client-side rendering library with no server component, no data collection, and no hosting surface. Foundational UI library used legitimately across millions of web applications.

FullContact.history: FullContact is a US identity resolution and contact enrichment platform founded in 2010 in Denver. It aggregates personal data to enrich contact records. The company has raised significant VC funding.
FullContact.summary_abuse: Moderate privacy and abuse concerns. FullContact aggregates personal data from various sources for enrichment, raising privacy concerns. It has been involved in data enrichment practices that some consider invasive.

fullPage.js.history: fullPage.js was created by Alvaro Trigo and first released in 2013. It became one of the most popular JavaScript plugins for creating fullscreen scrolling websites. It uses a dual-license model: free for open-source projects, paid for commercial use.
fullPage.js.summary_abuse: fullPage.js is a JavaScript scrolling library with no notable abuse signal. It is a pure frontend UI component.

Fullpath.history: Fullpath is an Israeli automotive retail AI and data platform founded in 2019. It provides customer data platform and AI-powered marketing for car dealerships. The company has raised significant VC funding.
Fullpath.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. An automotive-focused data and marketing platform. Collects first-party dealership data; privacy practices for automotive consumers should be reviewed.

FullSeats.history: FullSeats is a US event ticketing and venue management platform founded around 2020, targeting small venues and event organizers.
FullSeats.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A small ticketing platform; ticket scalping and fraud can affect any ticketing service but FullSeats has limited exposure.

FullStory.history: FullStory was founded in 2014 in Atlanta, Georgia by Scott Voigt, Bruce Johnson, and Joel Webber. The company raised $196M in total funding including a $103M round from investors including Google Ventures, Permira, and Salesforce Ventures, reaching a $1.8B unicorn valuation in 2021. The platform specializes in digital experience intelligence via session replay and analytics. Multiple class-action lawsuits were filed against FullStory customers for alleged unauthorized recording under wiretapping laws.
FullStory.summary_abuse: FullStory is a session replay and analytics tool embedded in third-party websites. Its script captures mouse movements, clicks, keystrokes, and page interactions. Multiple class-action lawsuits were filed in 2022-2023 against companies using FullStory, alleging that session replay constitutes unauthorized interception of electronic communications under the CIPA and similar laws. Research by Princeton and others showed session replay scripts can inadvertently capture passwords and financial data before form submission. FullStory offers a Private-by-Default mode that masks content, and has GDPR-compliant controls, but its deployment is often invisible to users. The tool itself is not directly abusive, but its passive data collection is a meaningful privacy risk factor when misconfigured by developers.

Funbutler Booking.history: Founded in 2014 in the Nordic region (Sweden/Norway) as a booking and operations system for activity centers and entertainment venues; market-leading in Scandinavia; no notable ownership changes.
Funbutler Booking.summary_abuse: Funbutler Booking is a niche activity-center booking platform with no notable abuse signal. It serves venues managing reservations and does not offer consumer-facing content hosting.

FunCaptcha.history: FunCaptcha was an Australian puzzle-based CAPTCHA service founded in 2013. It was acquired by Arkose Labs and rebranded. The technology is now marketed as Arkose Labs MatchKey.
FunCaptcha.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A CAPTCHA and bot detection service used to protect sites; has privacy implications from behavioral tracking.

Fundiin.history: Fundiin is a Vietnamese buy-now-pay-later fintech founded in 2019, providing installment payment solutions for Vietnamese e-commerce.
Fundiin.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. BNPL platforms can be used for payment fraud; Fundiin has KYC requirements in regulated fintech environment.

Funding Choices.history: Funding Choices is Google's consent management platform for publishers, launched in 2018. It helps publishers obtain user consent for personalized advertising under GDPR.
Funding Choices.summary_abuse: Low direct abuse risk. Managed by Google for consent management. The platform itself handles consent but relies on publishers correctly implementing it.

Fundraise Up.history: Fundraise Up is a US online fundraising platform for nonprofits founded in 2018, using AI to optimize donation conversion. The company raised VC funding and grew rapidly.
Fundraise Up.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A nonprofit fundraising platform with strong security practices and payment compliance.

FundRazr.history: FundRazr is a Canadian crowdfunding platform founded in 2009, supporting nonprofits, personal causes, and social campaigns.
FundRazr.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. Crowdfunding platforms are vulnerable to fundraising scams and fake charities. FundRazr has compliance procedures for campaign verification.

Funnel Science.history: Funnel Science is a US marketing analytics and lead generation platform founded around 2012, providing call tracking and conversion optimization for businesses.
Funnel Science.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. A call tracking and lead gen platform; some privacy concerns with tracking technologies.

FunnelCockpit.history: FunnelCockpit was founded in 2016 by Denis Caballero in Germany, positioning itself as the leading all-in-one sales funnel and marketing automation platform for the German-speaking DACH region. The platform is built in Germany with strict GDPR compliance and serves coaches, consultants, agencies, and SMEs. No major acquisitions or rebrands documented.
FunnelCockpit.summary_abuse: FunnelCockpit is a German all-in-one funnel builder targeting coaches and consultants in the DACH region. Like other funnel builder platforms, it can be used to create digital marketing campaigns, but its DACH focus and strict GDPR enforcement reduce the risk of anonymous mass-abuse. No documented abuse incidents.

Funnelforms.history: Founded in 2015 in Germany by Pascal and Fabian Zuhlsdorf as a WordPress plugin for intelligent multi-step form creation; no notable ownership changes since.
Funnelforms.summary_abuse: Funnelforms is a WordPress lead-capture form plugin with no notable abuse signal. Its embedded forms collect user data for site operators but it does not offer content hosting or anonymous account creation.

Funnelish.history: Funnelish is a sales funnel builder for ecommerce, originally created as a Shopify-friendly alternative to ClickFunnels. The company is bootstrapped with $1.8M in annual revenue as of 2025, operating with a 16-person team. It works as either a standalone ecommerce platform or as an extension layer on top of Shopify or WooCommerce. No major acquisitions.
Funnelish.summary_abuse: Funnelish is a sales funnel builder targeting ecommerce dropshippers and direct-response marketers. Funnel builder tools in the dropshipping space are occasionally used to set up fast-moving storefronts that sell counterfeit or misleading products, a risk common to all page builder tools in this segment. Funnelish itself has no documented specific abuse incidents, but its lightweight KYC and fast store creation create moderate abuse potential similar to competitors like ClickFunnels.

Funnels of Course.history: Funnels of Course is a US online course and sales funnel platform founded around 2021 targeting course creators and coaches.
Funnels of Course.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A niche online course platform with limited consumer-facing abuse exposure.

Funnelytics.history: Funnelytics is a visual funnel analytics and mapping platform founded around 2017. It allows marketers to build visual funnel diagrams and overlay real analytics data to track conversion performance. The company is based in Canada and bootstrapped, serving digital marketing agencies and solo marketers. No major acquisitions or rebrands.
Funnelytics.summary_abuse: Funnelytics is a B2B analytics and funnel visualization SaaS for marketing agencies and consultants. It has no notable abuse signal as it is a business analytics tool without consumer-facing hosting or anonymous account creation. No documented abuse incidents.

Funraise.history: Funraise is a US nonprofit fundraising platform founded in 2015, offering donation management and fundraising tools. The company raised VC funding.
Funraise.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A nonprofit fundraising platform with standard payment and security compliance.

FurnitureDealer.history: FurnitureDealer.net is a US e-commerce platform for furniture retailers founded around 2007, providing white-label online store solutions for the furniture industry.
FurnitureDealer.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A vertical e-commerce platform for furniture retailers with no notable abuse patterns.

Furo.history: Furo is a UK customer portal and authentication platform founded in 2021, providing white-label customer account management for SaaS companies.
Furo.summary_abuse: Low abuse risk. A B2B customer portal platform with no notable consumer-facing abuse patterns.

Fusion Ads.history: Fusion Ads is a Canadian advertising network for design and development communities, founded around 2008. It targets developer-focused publications and tools.
Fusion Ads.summary_abuse: Low-moderate abuse risk. An ad network; ad networks can serve malvertising, but Fusion Ads targets a niche developer audience with curated advertisers.

FusionCharts.history: FusionCharts was founded in 2002 by Pallav Nadhani and Alok Jain in Kolkata, India, initially building Flash-based charting components. The company transitioned entirely to JavaScript/HTML5 as Flash declined. It expanded internationally and has served over 500,000 developers across 120 countries. The company has remained independently owned and profitable without institutional funding.
FusionCharts.summary_abuse: FusionCharts is a commercial JavaScript charting library with no notable abuse history. As a developer-facing visualization toolkit with no consumer-facing surface or user-content hosting, it presents minimal abuse risk. The software is embedded into other applications and does not facilitate phishing, spam, or malware distribution.

Future Shop.history: Future Shop was a Canadian consumer electronics retailer founded in 1982 in Vancouver by Hassan Khosrowshahi. Best Buy acquired it in 2001 for approximately CAD $580 million. Best Buy initially maintained the brand alongside its own stores. In March 2015, Best Buy announced closure of all 131 Future Shop locations, converting 65 to Best Buy stores and shuttering the rest, ending the Future Shop brand permanently.
Future Shop.summary_abuse: Future Shop was a defunct Canadian electronics retailer brand shut down in 2015. As a historical retail platform, it no longer operates. When active, it faced standard e-commerce risks including phishing sites impersonating the brand and counterfeit goods. Since the brand's shutdown, there are no active abuse concerns related to its technology stack.

Futurio.history: Futurio is a WordPress theme first published around 2019. It gained popularity as a multipurpose theme offering Elementor page builder compatibility. The theme is distributed through the WordPress.org free theme directory and via a premium version on the developer's website. No notable acquisition or controversy events are on record.
Futurio.summary_abuse: Futurio is a WordPress multipurpose theme. Like all WordPress themes, it can be used to build any kind of website including spam or scam sites. However, the theme itself is not specifically associated with abuse. Standard WordPress ecosystem risks apply, including the theme being used as a base for counterfeit or phishing sites by third parties.

Futy.history: Futy is a French live chat and customer engagement SaaS platform founded around 2019. It targets small businesses offering website chat widgets and visitor engagement tools. No notable acquisitions or controversy events are documented.
Futy.summary_abuse: Futy is a small live chat SaaS with no notable abuse history. Like all live chat tools, it could theoretically be misused for spam or social engineering, but Futy itself has no documented history of enabling such activities at scale.

Fuzey Channels.history: Fuzey Channels is a UK-based customer communication platform founded around 2020, offering multi-channel messaging including SMS, WhatsApp, and email for small businesses. No notable acquisitions or major events are documented.
Fuzey Channels.summary_abuse: Fuzey Channels is a multi-channel messaging platform. Like all bulk messaging tools, it carries risk of being used for spam or unwanted marketing messages. The platform focuses on small business customer communications, and no specific abuse incidents have been publicly documented.

Fygaro.history: Fygaro is a Costa Rica-based e-commerce and payment link platform founded around 2018, primarily targeting Latin American small businesses and freelancers. It offers payment links, invoicing, and simple online stores without requiring technical knowledge.
Fygaro.summary_abuse: Fygaro is a payment link and e-commerce platform targeting Latin American markets. Like all payment processing tools, it could be used for fraudulent transactions. The free tier and ease of setup could attract bad actors, though no major publicly documented abuse incidents are known.

Fynd Platform.history: Fynd was founded in 2012 as Shopsense in Mumbai, India, and rebranded to Fynd. It started as an omnichannel retail platform connecting offline inventory to online channels. In 2019, Reliance Industries acquired a majority stake, providing significant backing. Fynd Platform evolved into a full e-commerce platform serving brands in India.
Fynd Platform.summary_abuse: Fynd Platform is an Indian e-commerce SaaS backed by Reliance Industries. As an e-commerce platform enabling merchants to set up online stores, it carries standard risks of being used for counterfeit goods or fraudulent storefronts. No major publicly documented abuse incidents have been reported.
